title: Metric components
summary: Expands the Poincaré normal form into radial–tangential blocks and proves that the displayed contravariant block is a left inverse when hⁱʲ is a left inverse of hᵢⱼ and r ≠ 0.

mathematicalContent.heading: Radial–tangential indices
mathematicalContent.body: An index I is either the radial direction r or a tangential index i. This allows every contraction to be split into one radial term and a sum over tangential directions.

mathematicalContent.heading: Metric and inverse blocks
mathematicalContent.body: For g₊ = r⁻²(dr²+hᵣ), the covariant matrix is r⁻² diag(1,hᵢⱼ), while the proposed contravariant matrix is r² diag(1,hⁱʲ). All mixed radial–tangential components vanish.

mathematicalContent.heading: Inverse identity
mathematicalContent.body: Assuming r is nonzero and hⁱᵏhₖⱼ = δⁱⱼ, Lean proves the full radial–tangential contraction g₊ⁱᴷg₊ₖⱼ = δⁱⱼ by cases on the two outer indices.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Creates the index set consisting of one radial index and all tangential indices from ι.
correspondence.leanType: inductive PEIndex (Iota : Type u) | radial | tangential (i : Iota)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the Kronecker delta on radial–tangential indices.
correspondence.leanType: def kronecker (I J : PEIndex Iota) : Real := if I = J then 1 else 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The diagonal value of the Kronecker delta is one.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_self (I : PEIndex Iota) : kronecker I I = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A radial index and a tangential index are distinct, so the corresponding Kronecker delta vanishes.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_radial_tangential (i : Iota) : kronecker radial (tangential i) = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The Kronecker delta also vanishes with a tangential first index and the radial second index.
correspondence.leanType: theorem kronecker_tangential_radial (i : Iota) : kronecker (tangential i) radial = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Identifies the radial index with none and each tangential index i with some i; this is a bijection with Option ι.
correspondence.leanType: def equivOption : PEIndex Iota ≃ Option Iota

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Splits a finite sum over PE indices into its single radial contribution and its tangential contributions.
correspondence.leanType: theorem sum_index (f : PEIndex Iota -> R) : sum I, f I = f radial + sum i, f (tangential i)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores a radius r, a tangential covariant block hᵢⱼ, and a proposed contravariant block hⁱʲ. Invertibility is not a structure field and must be supplied separately.
correspondence.leanType: structure PENormalMetricComponents (Iota : Type u) where r; h; hInv

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the covariant components of g₊: the radial entry is r⁻², mixed entries are zero, and the tangential block is r⁻²hᵢⱼ.
correspondence.leanType: def gPlusDown (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : PEIndex Iota -> PEIndex Iota -> Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the proposed contravariant components: the radial entry is r², mixed entries are zero, and the tangential block is r²hⁱʲ.
correspondence.leanType: def gPlusUp (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : PEIndex Iota -> PEIndex Iota -> Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds the radial–radial covariant component of the Poincaré metric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlusDown_radial_radial (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : gPlusDown C radial radial = (C.r^2)^(-1)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Unfolds the tangential covariant block of the Poincaré metric.
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlusDown_tangential_tangential (C) (i j) : gPlusDown C (tangential i) (tangential j) = (C.r^2)^(-1) * C.h i j
theorem gPlusUp_radial_radial (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : gPlusUp C radial radial = C.r^2
theorem gPlusUp_tangential_tangential (C) (i j) : gPlusUp C (tangential i) (tangential j) = C.r^2 * C.hInv i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: States that the proposed inverse boundary block hⁱʲ is a left inverse of hᵢⱼ.
correspondence.leanType: def BoundaryLeftInverse (C : PENormalMetricComponents Iota) : Prop := forall i j, sum k, C.hInv i k * C.h k j = if i = j then 1 else 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that the proposed contravariant matrix is a left inverse of the covariant Poincaré matrix. The proof uses r ≠ 0 to cancel r² with r⁻² and uses BoundaryLeftInverse in the tangential–tangential case.
correspondence.leanType: theorem gPlusUp_mul_gPlusDown (C) (hr : C.r != 0) (hbase : BoundaryLeftInverse C) (I J : PEIndex Iota) : sum K, gPlusUp C I K * gPlusDown C K J = kronecker I J

references.note: Background for the radial–tangential component form of a Poincaré metric.

limitations: The component theorem is an algebraic result for finite index sets. It assumes r ≠ 0 and assumes BoundaryLeftInverse; it does not construct hⁱʲ, prove positive-definiteness, or connect these arrays to the abstract MetricSymbol in NormalForm.lean.
